SocialProof.headline: 520+ hours saved per year for clients

0.body: Gabriel transformed how we work. He understood our business immediately and built a system that saves us hours every single day. We can finally focus on growth instead of just keeping up.
0.author: Zach Hodder

1.body: I reached out to Gabriel to help me set up my website and handle all the technical aspects. Gabriel deployed a fully personalized system for me, and he was completely attentive to my requests and requirements (for him, everything was clearly possible and achievable). He took charge of my current manual processes and administrative work. This saves me hours of work, making me more available to grow my business and reach more clients. Everything is clear and easy for me and my clients to understand. The result was more than up to my expectations. I recommend Gabriel with my eyes closed.
1.author: Elisa Curtelin

2.body: The automated workflows Gabriel implemented essentially doubled our team's capacity without hiring a single new person. We can now process 3x the volume of leads with zero added stress. Simply incredible.
2.author: Marc Dubois

About.headline: Former Entrepreneur. Software Engineer.
About.subheadline: I build the systems I wish I had when I was in your shoes.

story.title: From manual work to automated results
story.p1: I've always loved building businesses. From a childhood chess school to scaling ventures as a teenager, the game of entrepreneurship was my passion.
story.p2: But as my businesses grew, I hit a wall. I wasn't growing anymore—I was stuck doing paperwork, managing invoices, and chasing admin. I saw this everywhere, from the fitness industry to outdoor startups. Founders were being slowed down by their own success because their systems couldn't keep up.
story.p3: I decided to fix it. I became a software engineer to master the tools that scale companies. Today, I don't just write code; I build automated systems for businesses so owners can stop doing busywork and start making an impact. I build the systems I wish I had when I was starting out.

Legal.title: Legal Notice & Privacy Policy
Legal.metaDescription: Legal notice and privacy policy for gabrieldalmoro.com — GDPR compliant.
Legal.footerNote: This policy was last updated on March 6, 2026. For any questions, contact gabriel@gabrieldalmoro.com.

identity.title: Site Identity
identity.statusValue: Sole Proprietor, registered in Alberta, Canada

dataController.title: Data Controller
dataController.content: Gabriel Dalmoro is a sole proprietor registered in Alberta, Canada, currently operating from Annecy, France. He is the sole data controller for all personal data collected through this website. You can contact the data controller at gabriel@gabrieldalmoro.com.

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.intro: We only collect personal data that you provide voluntarily through the contact form on this website. This may include:
items: Your full name
Your professional email address
Information about your business and its needs (e.g., tools used, time spent on admin tasks)
Any additional message you provide voluntarily

purpose.title: Purpose & Legal Basis
purpose.intro: Your personal data is processed for the following purposes, on the following legal bases:
items: Responding to your enquiry — Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Evaluating if a business collaboration is possible — Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Sending a proposal or quote — Pre-contractual measures (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.content: Your data is retained for a maximum of 3 years from your last contact, in line with GDPR best practices and applicable data protection law. If no business relationship is established, contact data is deleted within 12 months of your initial message. You may request deletion at any time.

sharing.title: Data Sharing & Third Parties
sharing.intro: Your data is never sold. It may be shared only with the following third-party services strictly necessary for operating this website:
items: Vercel (hosting) — USA, covered by Standard Contractual Clauses
Google Workspace (email communication) — USA, covered by Standard Contractual Clauses
sharing.note: All third-party providers are selected for their GDPR compliance and reliability. No data is transferred to third countries without appropriate safeguards.

rights.title: Your Rights Under GDPR
rights.intro: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (EU 2016/679), you have the following rights:
items: Right of access — request a copy of the data held about you
Right to rectification — request correction of inaccurate data
Right to erasure — request deletion of your data ('right to be forgotten')
Right to restriction — request we limit how we use your data
Right to data portability — request your data in a portable format
Right to object — object to processing based on legitimate interests
Right to withdraw consent — where processing is based on consent
rights.exercise: To exercise any of these rights, contact the data controller directly:

cookies.title: Cookies & Analytics
cookies.content: This website uses Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights for anonymous, aggregated performance monitoring. No personal data is collected through these tools. Microsoft Clarity is used for anonymous session analysis to improve user experience. No cookies requiring consent are used. You may block analytics via your browser settings.

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.content: This website is hosted by Vercel Inc. Vercel processes data in accordance with EU Standard Contractual Clauses to ensure GDPR compliance for international data transfers.

complaints.title: Complaints & Supervisory Authority
complaints.content: If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the French data protection authority (CNIL), the competent supervisory authority for France:

Terms.title: Terms & Conditions
Terms.metaDescription: Terms and conditions governing the freelance services provided by Gabriel Dalmoro.
Terms.footerNote: These terms are governed by the laws of the Province of Alberta, Canada. Last updated: March 6, 2026.
Terms.intro: These Terms & Conditions govern the provision of freelance automation, software engineering, and related services by Gabriel Dalmoro ("the Service Provider") to clients ("the Client"). By engaging the Service Provider's services, you agree to be bound by these terms.

services.title: Services Provided
services.content: Gabriel Dalmoro offers business automation, custom software engineering, workflow design, system integration, and related digital services on a freelance basis. The specific scope, deliverables, timelines, and pricing for each engagement are agreed upon in writing prior to commencement of work (via proposal, quote, or statement of work).

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: These terms apply to all service engagements unless a separate written agreement is in place. By signing a proposal, paying a deposit, or instructing work to begin, the Client accepts these terms in full. If you do not agree, do not engage the services.

obligations.title: Client Obligations
obligations.intro: To ensure a successful engagement, the Client agrees to:
items: Provide timely, accurate, and complete information and access to systems required for the work
Designate a point of contact to provide feedback and approvals within agreed timeframes
Respect the agreed payment schedule
Not request changes outside the agreed scope without a written change order

payment.title: Pricing & Payment
payment.content: All prices are quoted in Euros (€) and are exclusive of any applicable VAT. Payment terms are as follows:
items: A deposit (typically 40%) is due before work begins
A midpoint payment (typically 30%) is due upon milestone delivery
The final payment (typically 30%) is due upon project completion
Invoices are due within 14 days of issue
Late payments will accrue interest at 2% per month (24% per annum), compounded monthly, as permitted under Alberta law

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.content: Upon receipt of full payment, the Client receives a full license to use all custom deliverables created for them. The Service Provider retains the right to use general knowledge, techniques, and methodologies. The Service Provider retains all rights to pre-existing tools, libraries, and frameworks used in the work. The Service Provider may mention the engagement in their portfolio unless the Client requests confidentiality in writing.

confidentiality.title: Confidentiality
confidentiality.content: Both parties agree to maintain the confidentiality of any sensitive business information shared during the engagement. The Service Provider will not disclose Client data, proprietary processes, or trade secrets to third parties. This obligation survives the end of the engagement.

guarantee.title: Results Guarantee
guarantee.content: For eligible automation projects, Gabriel Dalmoro guarantees that the delivered system will save the Client a minimum of 5 hours per week, as measured against the Client's baseline prior to the engagement. If this outcome is not achieved within 60 days of delivery through no fault of the Client, the Service Provider will continue to improve and optimise the system at no additional charge until the guarantee is met. This guarantee applies only to the agreed scope of automation and requires that the Client's environment and processes remain substantially unchanged.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content: The Service Provider's total liability for any claim arising from an engagement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client for that engagement. The Service Provider is not liable for indirect, consequential, or incidental damages, including loss of business, loss of data, or lost profits. The Service Provider is not responsible for failures caused by third-party services, client infrastructure, or changes in the client's environment.

termination.title: Termination
termination.content: Either party may terminate an engagement by providing 14 days' written notice. In the event of termination, the Client will be invoiced for all work completed up to the date of termination. Deposits are non-refundable unless the Service Provider fails to commence the agreed work. The Service Provider may terminate immediately in the event of non-payment exceeding 30 days or a material breach of these terms.

law.title: Governing Law & Jurisdiction
law.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Province of Alberta and the applicable federal laws of Canada, without regard to conflict of law principles. In the event of a dispute that cannot be resolved amicably, the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta, Canada. Notwithstanding the foregoing, clients based in the European Union retain all mandatory rights under their applicable local consumer protection law, which these Terms do not seek to override.

modifications.title: Modifications
modifications.content: The Service Provider reserves the right to update these Terms at any time. The version in effect at the time of an engagement's commencement governs that specific engagement. Clients are encouraged to review these terms before entering into a new agreement.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For any questions regarding these Terms & Conditions, please contact:
